commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And if it be of an unclean animal, then he shall redeem it according to your valuation, and shall add a fifth part to it: or if it be not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to your valuation. Unclean, either on account of some blemish, or because it is of those species which cannot be sacrificed; such as the horse, camel, which might nevertheless be vowed to the Lord, and sold for the benefit of his priests. Moses and the succeeding priests. Many manuscripts read, with the Septuagint and Chaldean, "by him "leaving the matter to the person's conscience; but the printed Hebrew and Vulgate agree.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
